description: Recommended authoritative sources for accessibility and design system content. Sources are prioritized by reliability level.

gold_standard.title: WCAG 2.1 Quick Reference
gold_standard.notes: The definitive accessibility guidelines - should always be referenced for compliance questions

gold_standard.title: HTML Living Standard
gold_standard.notes: Defines native element semantics - critical for understanding why semantic HTML is preferred

authoritative.title: Inclusive Components
authoritative.notes: Heydon Pickering's extensively tested accessible component patterns. Prioritizes semantic HTML over ARIA. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED as APG alternative.

authoritative.title: GOV.UK Design System
authoritative.notes: UK Government Design System - extensively tested with real users including those using assistive technology. Gold standard for tested patterns.

authoritative.title: Deque University Resources
authoritative.notes: Industry-leading accessibility training and patterns from the creators of axe-core.

authoritative.title: The A11Y Project
authoritative.notes: Community-driven accessibility resource with practical, vetted guidance.

authoritative.title: Adrian Roselli's Blog
authoritative.notes: Adrian Roselli's accessibility expertise - often critiques APG examples and provides better alternatives.

authoritative.title: Scott O'Hara's Blog
authoritative.notes: Scott O'Hara's accessibility research - provides deep dives into accessible component implementations.

reference.title: ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
reference.notes: IMPORTANT CAVEATS: This is a reference for ARIA mechanics, NOT a gold standard for accessibility. Always prefer semantic HTML. Test with assistive technology before using these patterns.

caveats: APG demonstrates ARIA usage patterns, NOT complete accessibility solutions
Native HTML elements should ALWAYS be preferred over ARIA implementations
Some APG examples have known issues with certain assistive technologies
Always test with actual screen readers (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver) before production use

ingestion_notes.priority_order: Ingest gold_standard and authoritative sources first. Reference sources should be marked with appropriate caveats.
ingestion_notes.content_enrichment: When ingesting, ensure source reliability metadata is properly set based on the reliability_override if provided.
ingestion_notes.update_frequency: Gold standard sources should be checked for updates quarterly. Authoritative sources monthly. Community sources as needed.
